title: Who is Manoj Agarwal? West Bengal chief electoral officer appointed as chief secretary by Suvendu Adhikari government
content:
Manoj Agarwal has been appointed as the Chief Secretary for West Bengal.
*   He previously held the position of West Bengal's Chief Electoral Officer.
*   The appointment was reportedly made by the "Suvendu Adhikari government," leading the headline to question his identity.

title: HSBC slashes FY27 GDP estimate sharply to 6%, expects two RBI rate hikes
content:
HSBC has sharply reduced its GDP growth estimate for FY27.
*   The new forecast for FY27 GDP growth now stands at 6%.
*   The bank also anticipates two interest rate hikes by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI).

title: Jewellery sector bats for gold monetisation scheme
content:
The jewellery sector is actively advocating for.
*   Their support is directed towards the Gold Monetisation Scheme.
*   This indicates the industry sees potential benefits or advantages in the scheme.

title: No bailout package for OMCs in sight as under-recoveries mount
content:
Oil Marketing Companies (OMCs) are facing significant and growing financial losses.
*   There is currently no government bailout package in sight for these companies.
*   These losses stem from "under-recoveries," meaning OMCs sell fuel at prices below their acquisition and operating costs.

title: PM Modi welcomes move to implement Ayushman Bharat scheme in West Bengal
content:
Here are 3 bullet points summarizing the headline:

*   PM Modi has expressed his welcome and approval.
*   The Ayushman Bharat scheme is now set to be implemented in West Bengal.
*   This signifies the rollout of the national health insurance program in the state.

title: GBA polls: Karnataka SEC appeals to Supreme Court for extension of deadline  by 3 months
content:
The Karnataka State Election Commission (SEC) has appealed to the Supreme Court.
*   They are requesting an extension for a current deadline.
*   The SEC is asking for a 3-month extension specifically related to the GBA polls.

title: Will hear Trinamool pleas on victory margins being less than deleted votes in SIR, says Supreme Court
content:
The Supreme Court will hear pleas from the Trinamool Congress.
*   Trinamool's concern is about election victory margins being less than the number of votes deleted.
*   The pleas relate to these discrepancies found in the Scrutiny and Investigation Report (SIR), implying a challenge to the validity of the results.

title: Supreme Court places Sringeri recount result in abeyance; restores Congress leader Raje Gowda as MLA for now
content:
The Supreme Court has suspended the recount result for the Sringeri constituency.
*   Congress leader Raje Gowda has been reinstated as an MLA.
*   Raje Gowda's restoration to MLA status is temporary, or "for now."

title: Clamour for mini fire station at Ramanattukara  grows louder
content:
There is a strong public demand for a mini fire station in Ramanattukara.
*   The call for establishing this facility has significantly intensified.
*   The community's advocacy for improved local emergency services is becoming more vocal and urgent.
